ticket.title: FIX INCORRECT DESTINATION RESOLUTION ON GDX BOOKING LOOKUP

test_cases.description: Verify all 7 Puerto Princesa bookings (GDX 19395, 21285, 19817, 19384, 20985, 19481, 20146) resolve to Puerto Princesa, not Boracay, on the domestic booking lookup.
test_cases.expected_result: Destination field returns 'Puerto Princesa' for all 7 bookings.
test_cases.actual_result: All 7 bookings (Severino, Toledo, Basilan, Santos, Somera-Arrobang, Mendoza, Ligayo) resolved to 'Boracay' instead of 'Puerto Princesa'. 0/7 passed.
test_cases.comments: Currently all 7 resolve to Boracay - 0/7 passed in prior cycle.

test_cases.description: Verify GDX 21954 (Burlat), expected under Boracay, resolves to Boracay and not Puerto Princesa.
Verify GDX 21851 (Chua), expected under Cebu, resolves to Cebu and not Boracay.
test_cases.actual_result: Resolved to 'Boracay' instead of 'Cebu'. Date field also corrupted (see TC-Date Corruption sheet).

test_cases.description: Spot-check a sample of previously passing bookings across Boracay, El Nido, Cebu, Siargao, Bohol, and Davao to confirm the destination fix has no regression.
test_cases.expected_result: All spot-checked bookings continue to resolve to their correct destination.
test_cases.actual_result: Not yet re-tested; fix has not been deployed.

test_cases.description: Verify the underlying cause: check whether the destination-mapping logic (booking ID to destination table/lookup) has a shared root cause across the affected bookings (Boracay <-> Puerto Princesa <-> Cebu cross-contamination).
test_cases.expected_result: Root cause identified and documented; fix should be applied at the mapping/data layer, not per-booking.
test_cases.actual_result: Not yet investigated by dev team; pattern across 3 separate destination groups suggests a shared/systemic mapping bug rather than isolated data errors.
test_cases.comments: Dev investigation - likely systemic destination ID/index mismatch.
